user_message: Working on Contoso Ltd's security posture. Got the escalation email -- help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 15:37 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 11:52 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Contoso Ltd

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 10:37 EST
Subject: Re: Re: security posture Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-K trap (Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-K failure mode (Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-K -- Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
